title: OscoreSeqNum::increment wraps silently in release builds at u32::MAX
description:
File: rust/lichen-oscore/src/seqnum.rs
Line: 57

The increment() method uses wrapping_add which silently wraps to 0 in release builds when sequence number is at u32::MAX. While Context::protect_request guards against this by checking before increment, direct use of increment() is unsafe.

Code:
```rust
pub fn increment(self) -> Self {
    debug_assert!(self.0 < u32::MAX, ...);
    Self(self.0.wrapping_add(1))  // wraps silently in release
}
```

Security impact: Nonce reuse causes complete loss of OSCORE confidentiality (AES-CCM keystream reuse) and potentially auth bypass.

Fix: Return Option<Self> or Result, or use checked_add and panic on overflow in release builds too. The doc comment acknowledges this is a security violation but doesn't prevent it.
close_reason: increment now returns Option with checked_add; tests cover MAX case
